Bendigo & Adelaide Bank Target Price Cut 4.3% to A$11.00/Share by UBS>BEN.AU
India bond yields seen steady before key events
data.description:
Indian government bonds are expected to trade largely unchanged in early deals on Tuesday, as investors await key market-moving events, including debt purchase by the central bank and Federal Reserve minutes, in the second half of the week.

The benchmark 10-year yield (IN067934G=CC) is likely to move between 6.68% and 6.70%, a trader with a private bank said, compared with its previous close of 6.6930%. Indian debt markets will be closed on Wednesday for a local holiday.

"We should see sideway moves today, as immediately there is nothing that could move the markets on either side," the trader said. "There should be some consolidation in the 6.68%-6.70% zone."

The Reserve Bank of India will buy bonds worth 400 billion rupees ($4.60 billion) on Thursday, which will be the last leg of its mega liquidity infusion package announced towards the end of January.

For this auction, the central bank has also included the benchmark 6.79% 2034 security. It bought around 50 billion rupees of this paper in its first debt purchase in January, but did not include it in the second auction.

The RBI has so far bought bonds worth 600 billion rupees via auctions and another 388.15 billion rupees through secondary market screen-based purchases in January.

It has injected around 440 billion rupees through a dollar/rupee buy/sell swap and 1.82 trillion rupees through four-day to 56-day repos. The 3.25 trillion-rupee infusion in the banking system has taken place over the last five weeks.

The RBI has also been providing funds through daily overnight repos and adjusting the quantum based on the banking system's requirement, which has helped to keep the overnight rates closer to the policy rate.

Minutes of the Federal Reserve's last policy meeting are due on Wednesday, followed by those from the RBI on Friday. The RBI cut policy rate by 25 basis points for the first time in nearly five years on February 7.

Indian states will sell bonds later in the day, while New Delhi will sell debt on Friday.

KEY INDICATORS:

** Brent crude futures were down 0.1% at $75.15 per barrel, after gaining 0.6% in the previous session

** Ten-year U.S. Treasury yield at 4.5074%; two-year yield (US2YT=RR) at 4.2694%

** Twelve Indian states to raise 298 billion rupees via sale of bonds

** RBI to conduct 1-day variable rate repo auction for 1 trillion rupees

($1 = 86.9180 Indian rupees)

data.title: GPT Target Price Raised 1.3% to A$5.29/Share by UBS>GPT.AU
Geely Automobile jumps after parent's deal with Renault to produce car in Brazil
data.description:
** Shares of Geely Automobile jump 6.3% to HK$17.94, their highest since Feb. 10

** Stock on track for the biggest one-day gain since Feb. 12; top gainer in Hang Seng Index , which rises 1.4%

** French automaker Renault and China's Zhejiang Geely Holding (GEELY.UL) said in a joint statement on Monday that they have reached an agreement to produce and sell zero- and low-emission vehicles in Brazil

** Geely Automobile, in which Zhejiang Geely holds an indirect 39% stake, said on Tuesday that it was not a party to the agreement and is neither partnering with nor providing any financing to Zhejiang Geely

** "Given the potential synergy from the collaboration with Renault and the significant potential of the Latin American market, the group is actively exploring different options to be involved in the proposed transaction," Geely Automobile said

** Hang Seng Commerce & Industry Index (.HSNC) rises 1.5% and Hang Seng Tech Index climbs 1.4%

** Geely Automobile shares rose 72.7% last year

data.title: ASX Midday Sector Update: Healthcare Sector Rises, Energy Stocks Plunge
data.description:
Healthcare stocks advanced 0.3% at midday Tuesday. The Reserve Bank of Australia is expected to announce the anticipated interest rate decision later on the same day.

Opthea completed the final week-52 patient visit in Coast, the first of two phase three trials evaluating a combination therapy candidate for wet age-related macular degeneration, an eye disease that can blur vision.

On the other hand, energy stocks were down 1.7%. Woodside Energy Group extended losses from the Monday trading session, after it implied its final dividend could fall below expectations by as much as 20%.

Woodside held talks with several potential buyers of stakes in its Louisiana liquefied natural gas plant, including Tokyo Gas, Japan's JERA, and MidOcean Energy, Reuters reported citing multiple sources.

data.title: China Stocks Fall on Profit-Taking
data.description:
The Shanghai Composite dipped 0.25% to fall below 3,350, while the Shenzhen Component lost 0.2% to 10,772 on Tuesday, as mainland stocks paused their recent rally driven by excitement over DeepSeek’s artificial intelligence capabilities.

Investors took the opportunity to lock in profits after the recent surge.

Market participants also assessed the outlook following President Xi Jinping’s pledge to support the private sector in a closed-door meeting with prominent business leaders.

Xi urged the business community to demonstrate their talent and confidence in China’s model and market as global competition intensifies.

This marks a shift from the years-long regulatory crackdown that had depressed equity valuations.

Notable losses were seen from Beijing Enlight (-8.9%), China United Network (-3.7%), IEIT Systems (-4.6%), Shanghai Athub (-10%), and East Money Information (-1.9%).

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

2137 ET - Bermaz Auto could face significant challenges as its car offerings, particularly Mazda and Kia, struggle with declining sales, Affin Hwang IB says in a note. The introduction of more affordable alternatives from Chinese brands and RON95 fuel-subsidy reforms, which could weigh on consumer spending, lead her to cut the Malaysian auto distributor's FY 2025-FY 2027 core EPS forecasts by 17%-29%. Weak demand for certain Kia models and continuing promotions are also pressuring margins, she adds. Affin Hwang reduces Bermaz Auto's target price to MYR1.05 from MYR1.89 while maintaining a sell rating on the stock. Shares are 0.8% lower at MYR1.25. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2135 ET - Jefferies analysts aren't ruling out more copper M&A for BHP, even after the miner's CEO, Mike Henry, said the company is firmly focused on its own projects. "We reiterate our hold rating on BHP as the company has been more aggressive than its peers in pursuing opportunistic M&A, and we believe further M&A risk remains," the Jefferies analysts say in a note. They also raise concerns about the outlook for dividends unless commodity prices surprise. "Combined with its rising net debt and organic growth pipeline, BHP's ability to deliver capital returns in excess of its base 50% payout ratio is limited unless commodity prices are much stronger than we anticipate," the analysts say. Jefferies has a A$43.00 target on BHP, which is up 0.5% at A$41.01/share.(rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2129 ET - Seatrium's order book faces upside risks from President Trump's focus on U.S. energy dominance, OCBC Investment Research's Ada Lim says in a research report. Trump has laid out a plan to unleash U.S. oil and gas production and boost output, which includes promoting oil and gas development in Alaska, the analyst notes. Such policy developments could prompt global oil companies to increase oil and gas capital expenditures, potentially enabling Seatrium's order book to surprise on the upside, the analyst says. OCBC raises the stock's fair value estimate to S$2.82 from S$2.64 to reflect a valuation roll-forward, with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 0.4% higher at S$2.58. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2105 ET - Chinese shares edge lower in early trade with the Shanghai Composite Index down 0.3%. The Shenzhen Composite Index is 0.3% lower and ChiNext Price Index is off 0.2%. Investors are waiting for China's key political meeting in early March and more economic data. Among major stocks, retail stocks are leading the declines, with Yonghui Superstores dropping 3.4%. China Tourism Group Duty Free declines 1.1%. Gainers include BYD, which adds 1.4% and CATL, which gains 1.0%. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

2103 ET - Johor Plantations' earnings outlook may stay robust, supported by stronger average selling prices for palm oil and manageable unit costs, RHB IB analyst Hoe Lee Leng says in a note. She raises Johor Plantations' 2025-2026 earnings estimates by 5% and 7%, respectively, after robust 4Q 2024 earnings. The plantation company remains an appealing investment option, trading at a relatively attractive 2025 price-to-earnings ratio of 11X, which is at the mid-point of its peers' 8X-16X, she adds. RHB raises Johor Plantations' target price to MYR1.65 from MYR1.55, while maintaining a buy rating on the stock. Shares are 0.8% higher at MYR1.21. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2053 ET - Hong Kong's benchmark Hang Seng Index rises 0.8% to 22800.86, led by tech stocks. Chinese President Xi's meeting with top tech CEOs where he urged the business leaders to stay competitive and confident in the future of the country, sends a strong signal on China's commitment to boost the economy, says the Saxo APAC Research team in a commentary. Xiaomi is up 3.0%, BYD gains 2.1% and Alibaba Group adds 1.9%. The Hang Seng Tech Index is 1.4% higher. Among decliners, Henderson Land Development falls 1.8%, Wharf Real Estate Investment is down 1.5% and China Resources Land is 1.0% lower. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

2049 ET - Seek's first-half result is potentially higher quality than anticipated by the market even though earnings are slightly lower than the average analyst forecast, E&P analyst Entcho Raykovski says. The Australian job advertiser's December-half Ebitda of A$233.9 million compares with an average forecast of A$231.2 million, although it is in line with Raykovski's forecast. He writes in a note that the company's updated Ebitda guidance is in line with expectations despite some shift in spending from capital expenditure to operating expenses. With yield growth guidance upgraded, Raykovski sees reasons to be positive on the quality of the result. E&P has a last-published positive rating and A$28.30 target price on the stock, which is flat at A$24.23. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

2040 ET - CapitaLand Investment's share price may rebound to S$2.68, a swing high resistance level formed at the beginning of January 2025, Phillip Securities Research's Zane Aw says in a commentary. The share price on Monday broke out of a downtrend channel within which it had been trading since November 2024, the analyst notes. Also, the current Bollinger bands' squeeze suggests underlying market is undergoing a low-volatility period, which is typically followed by a high-volatility period, the analyst says. This gives a potential signal for the share price to revert to a bullish trend. Moreover, the moving average convergence divergence indicator supports the bullish view, with increasing momentum, the analyst adds. Shares are 0.8% lower at S$2.52. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2033 ET - Audinate's bulls at Morgan Stanley are happy with the audio-tech provider's insistence that current growth headwinds are cyclical rather than structural. The MS analysts tell clients in a note that the Australia-listed company's first-half results provide comfort that underlying adoption trends remain strong. They point to 31% growth in software unit sales despite customer destocking of chip, card and module inventory. Audinate also seems to be showing good cost control through what they call a challenging destocking period. MS lifts its target price 22% to A$11.00 and stays overweight on the stock, which is up 6.7% at A$10.23. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

2026 ET - Eco World Development's recent sale of a 138.5-acre plot of land in Iskandar Malaysia to Microsoft for 694 million ringgit monetizes the asset and strengthens the developer's balance sheet, Maybank IB analyst Wong Wei Sum says in a note. The deal, which is 52% higher than a prior sale to Microsoft, demonstrates the property developer's ability to secure value-enhancing deals amid data-center demand uncertainty, she says. Eco World's improved balance sheet should position it well for future land acquisitions and sustained earnings growth, she adds. Maybank raises Eco World's target price to MYR2.34 from MYR2.33 and maintains a buy rating on the stock. Shares are 2.2% higher at MYR1.88. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2017 ET - Singapore's FTSE Straits Times Index edges 0.1% lower to 3900.87, erasing opening gains as local market tracks losses in most regional equity markets. Chinese President Xi met with some prominent domestic entrepreneurs Monday in a show of state support for the private sector but there was little by way of specific policy announcements, says Alvin T. Tan, head of Asia FX Strategy at RBC Capital Markets. Among the worst performers on Singapore's benchmark index, Genting Singapore falls 1.9% and Hongkong Land drops 1.8%. Meanwhile, SATS Ltd. rises 0.9% and Singtel is 0.6% higher. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2011 ET - Far East Hospitality Trust could face headwinds from a slowdown in Singapore's tourism sector, Citi Research analyst Brandon Lee says in a note. The sector's growth could moderate further this year, he writes. Citing the Singapore Tourism Board, Lee notes that international visitors are expected to reach between 17 million and 18.5 million this year. That implies 3% to 12% growth compared to the 21% expansion seen in 2024, he says. Citi downgrades the hospitality-focused REIT's rating to sell from buy and reduces its target price to S$0.53 from S$0.73. Units are 1.7% lower at S$0.59. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.description:
India's Cube Highways Trust plans to raise 6 billion rupees ($69.03 million) selling bonds maturing in 19 years and one month, three bankers said on Tuesday.

The company has invited bids from bankers and investors for the issue on Thursday, they said.

The bonds have a put/call option at the end of five years.

The company did not reply to a Reuters email seeking comment.

data.title: Audinate Target Price Raised 2.4% to A$10.85/Share by UBS>AD8.AU
Maturity of NDF positions, dip in Asian peers likely to weigh on rupee
data.description:
The Indian rupee is likely to face pressure on Tuesday due to heightened dollar demand spurred by the maturity of positions in the non-deliverable forwards (NDF) market, while a dip in regional peers and portfolio outflows may add to the headwinds.

The 1-month non-deliverable forward indicated that the rupee will open at 86.93-86.94 to the U.S. dollar, compared with 86.8775 in the previous session.

Bids to buy dollars at the daily reference rate are likely to be elevated due to the maturity of large positions in the NDF market, three traders said.

The cumulative size of the maturities is estimated to be around $4 billion, a Singapore-based trader at a bank said.

A decline in Asian currencies is also likely to weigh on the rupee with its peers down between 0.1% to 0.3%.

The dollar index was up 0.1% at 106.9, recovering slightly from its lowest level in about two months.

"Unless you have a strong conviction that the U.S. activity data is going to decelerate sharply from here... it looks like we're getting towards the end of this dollar correction," ING Bank said in a note.

Meanwhile, persistent foreign portfolio outflows have also hurt the rupee, with overseas investors having net pulled out about $12 billion from local stocks in 2025 so far.

The rupee has weakened about 1.5% year-to-date and is among the worst performing regional currencies.

It declined to an all-time low of 87.95 last week and might have had to endure deeper losses had it not been for the Reserve Bank of India's stern interventions, which have blunted speculative bets against the currency.

Traders expect the central bank to continue stepping into the market to prevent excessive volatility.

KEY INDICATORS:

** One-month non-deliverable rupee (INRNDFOR=) forward at 87.12; onshore one-month forward premium at 19.50 paisa

** Dollar index up 0.1% at 106.87

** Brent crude futures down 0.2% at $75.1 per barrel

** Ten-year U.S. note yield at 4.51%

** As per NSDL data, foreign investors sold a net $578.2 million worth of Indian shares on February 14

** NSDL data shows foreign investors sold a net $30.6 million worth of Indian bonds on February 14

data.title: Rate Cuts Would Support Capital Flows Into Funds Management Business, Dexus CEO Says >DXS.AU
Dexus Sees Rate Cuts as Positive for Sentiment Toward Property, Deal Flow — Interview
data.description:
(02:40 GMT) *Dexus CEO Says Rate Cuts Would Be Positive for Sentiment Toward Property, Deal Flow >DXS.AU
(02:40 GMT) *Rate Cuts Would Support Capital Flows Into Funds Management Business, Dexus CEO Says >DXS.AU
(02:40 GMT) *Asset Selection Has Never Been More Important Than Now, Dexus CEO Says >DXS.AU
(02:40 GMT) Dexus Sees Rate Cuts as Positive for Sentiment Toward Property, Deal Flow — Interview

By David Winning

SYDNEY--Interest-rate cuts should be positive for investor sentiment toward Australian property and accelerate deal flow, although not all assets will benefit to the same degree, Dexus Chief Executive Ross Du Vernet said.

The aftermath of the Covid-19 pandemic has been a challenging time for many owners of Australian property, with elevated interest rates adding to challenges that include shifts in workplace behavior toward more working from home and fragile consumer confidence weighing on retail sales.

Those headwinds have left their mark on asset valuations, particularly office towers, and made some investors reluctant to become more active in the real-estate sector.

However, money markets are signaling that Australia's central bank will join global counterparts in cutting interest rates at the conclusion of its two-day meeting later on Tuesday. Most economists think the Reserve Bank of Australia will lower the cash rate by 25 basis points to 4.10%, representing the first reduction since 2020, although they are divided over how deep and rapid the loosening cycle will be given global trade concerns and the risk of inflation heating up again.

"A rate cut will be good for high-quality assets like in our portfolio," Du Vernet said in an interview. "The bigger impact for us is deal flow and capital flows into our funds-management business."

While lower interest rates would give the sector a boost, Du Vernet said some real-estate assets considered to be of a lower quality may not benefit much. "The fundamentals of an asset would continue to weigh on it," he said.

Dexus said on Tuesday it is in the process of reviewing opportunities to launch new products as it reported a net profit of 10.3 million Australian dollars (US$6.5 million) for the six months through December, reversing a A$597.2 million loss at the same stage of a year earlier. It kept its annual guidance for funds from operations and its distribution unchanged.

Du Vernet said there's been a noticeable uptick in enquiries from investors in wanting to do new things, suggesting a desire to get ahead of interest rates coming down. For many investors, property has been under-represented in their portfolios in recent years, so they are playing catch-up to some degree, he said.

Still, pension funds that are typically cautious will need to be confident that the interest-rate cycle has turned, Du Vernet said.

"One thing that we have observed both in data and investment preferences is that asset selection has never been more important than now," said Du Vernet, drawing a contrast to the past decade when investors latched on to themes when deploying capital. "Asset selection and asset management is much more important to performance and clients are attuned to this."

Write to David Winning at david.winning@wsj.com

data.title: Dexus CEO Says Rate Cuts Would Be Positive for Sentiment Toward Property, Deal Flow >DXS.AU
Bull Sees China Headwinds Fade With a2 Milk's Gains — Market Talk
data.description: The a2 Milk Company's bull at Citi sees the infant-formula maker's December-half result as more evidence that headwinds in China against overseas providers have dissipated. Gone are the days in which market-share gains are dominated by domestic brands, analyst Sam Teeger says. Reiterating his buy recommendation, Teeger tells clients in a note that a2 Milk is the third highest market-share gainer in China, trailing Nestle and China's Yili. Three of the top five brands are now from outside China, he observes. He sees a2 Milk gearing up for a strong fiscal 2026, with share-price catalysts including potential government stimulus, supply chain acquisitions, and capital management. Citi raises its target price 12% to A$8.20. Shares are up 2.7% at A$7.315. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

GuocoLand Unit Prices SG$180 Million Perpetual Securities
data.description:
GuocoLand subsidiary GLL IHT priced its offering of SG$180 million worth of 4.35% perpetual securities under its SG$3.0 billion multicurrency medium-term bond program.

DBS Bank, HSBC, and OCBC have been appointed joint lead managers and book-runners for the offering, according to a Monday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The bonds are expected to be issued on or about Feb. 25.

data.title: Samsung Electronics nominates chip execs as new board members
data.description:
Samsung Electronics on Tuesday nominated its chip business chief Jun Young-hyun and Chief Technology Officer Song Jai-hyuk to join its board, as the tech giant looks to boost competitiveness in its struggling semiconductor business.

The South Korean company also said in a regulatory filing it had nominated Seoul National University Professor Lee Hyuk-jae as an outside director. Lee, a chip expert, is head of Seoul National University's semiconductor research centre.

With two chip executives and one semiconductor academic nominated to the board, Samsung is looking to strengthen its focus on chips at the top level of the company.

Samsung Electronics has been scrambling to restore its competitiveness in the chip business, after it lost market leadership in high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips used in Nvidia's AI graphics processing units (GPUs) to domestic rival SK Hynix .

Samsung said the new board nominations would be voted on at a shareholders' meeting slated for March 19.

data.title: SOUEAST Expands Into the Middle East: A Bold New Chapter
data.description:
WUHU, Anhui, Feb. 17, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — After successful launch events in Iraq, Kuwait, the UAE, and Qatar, SOUEAST has officially entered the Middle Eastern automotive market. The brand is set to make its debut in Saudi Arabia, the region’s largest market, in the second quarter of 2025.

SOUEAST aims to deliver high-quality vehicles that combine cutting-edge design, powerful performance, and reliable quality tailored exclusively to the needs of Middle Eastern drivers.

A Fresh Vision for Mobility

Established in 1995, SOUEAST is one of the pioneers among Chinese automotive brands. With over 20 years of global market experience, the brand is entering an exciting new phase of development. The name "SOUEAST" blends “Soul” and “Ease,” reflecting its core mission: enable more youth around the world to have an ease urban mobility experience.

SOUEAST’s philosophy, “Ease Your Life,” inspires every aspect of its lineup—seamlessly integrating convenience, performance, and style into the lives of urban drivers. The brand is committed to creating vehicles that enhance daily experiences, making life on the move as stress-free and enjoyable as possible.

Designed for a New Generation

SOUEAST’s target audience includes young professionals and families aged 25 to 40—drivers who are dynamic, adventurous, and passionate about quality and style. These consumers seek vehicles that reflect their ambitions and complement their lifestyles.

The brand aims to meet these needs by offering vehicles that combine practicality, cutting-edge technology, and bold design. SOUEAST strives to offer a driving experience that helps people explore, connect, and thrive.

Innovative Vehicles for the Saudi Market

In 2025, SOUEAST will introduce a range of vehicles specifically designed for Saudi consumers. Key models include the S09 Smart Premium SUV, a premium 7-seater designed for urban elites, combining business functionality with family-friendly features. This model is equipped with a turbocharged engine, all-wheel drive, and a luxurious interior, offering a balance of power and comfort.

The S07 Urban Intelligent SUV stands as a stylish, tech-savvy SUV tailored for young professionals, with advanced smart features, a spacious design, and dynamic performance for both daily commutes and weekend adventures.

Finally, the S06 Urban Stylish SUV, a fashion and sporty model full of character. For those looking for a tech-forward driving experience, this model is an ideal choice.

Each model boasts modern aesthetics, intelligent technologies, and premium comfort features that set them apart in their class. Full specifications and pricing will be announced closer to the launch date.

A Commitment to Saudi Arabia

SOUEAST recognizes the strategic importance of Saudi Arabia within the Middle East and is fully committed to the market. By the end of 2025, the brand plans to open exclusive showrooms across the country, enhancing accessibility for customers.

To ensure a seamless ownership experience, SOUEAST will establish a comprehensive after-sales and spare parts center, guaranteeing fast and reliable service in the region. From purchase to maintenance, Saudi drivers can expect a one-stop solution designed for convenience and peace of mind.

The Middle Eastern launch is not the beginning of SOUEAST’s global expansion plan, as the brand had already held a global brand launch in Uzbekistan in September 2024. Following its success in the region, the brand will continue to expand into more countries in Central Asia and the Middle East, as well as other regions around the world, bringing its innovative vehicles to drivers worldwide in the coming years.”

Stay Connected

For more details on SOUEAST’s upcoming launches in Saudi Arabia, visit the official website at https://soueastksa.com.

Company Name: SOUEAST MOTOR

Contact Person: Frida Fu

Email: yuhe.fu@soueast-motor.com

State: AnHui

Country: China

Website: https://www.soueast-motor.com/

A photo accompanying this announcement is available at https://www.globenewswire.com/NewsRoom/AttachmentNg/c17ffd5c-d492-4089-be94-2156ec0532dc

data.title: Shanghai STEP Electric Surges 10% After Haier Takes Controlling Stake
data.description:
Shanghai STEP Electric shares surged 10% after Haier CoaXus Industrial Intelligence agreed to take a controlling stake in the industrial robot manufacturer.

Ji Defa, Liu Liping, and Ji Yi on Feb. 14 signed an agreement to transfer 66,306,129 shares in the company to Haier CoaXus Industrial Intelligence, according to a Monday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

They also signed voting rights entrustment and acting-in-concert agreements, entrusting the voting rights of their remaining 127,583,569 shares to Haier CoaXus Industrial Intelligence.

As a result, Haier CoaXus Industrial Intelligence will control voting rights for 193,889,698 shares, or 29.2% of the total, making it the new controlling shareholder, with Haier Group as the ultimate controlling entity.

Following a planned private share issuance, Haier CoaXus Industrial Intelligence is expected to hold 218,810,226 shares of Shanghai STEP Electric.
